source_issue.title: Backend instructions should explain how to add add-ons

summary_markdown:
```markdown
## What this issue is about
This issue addresses a gap in the Plone documentation for version 6.x, specifically regarding how to properly add add-ons to the backend. Currently, the only method described is using the `ADDONS` variable, which is not recommended for production environments.

## What needs to be done
We need to enhance the documentation to clarify how to add add-ons effectively, using a proper method that is suitable for production. The suggestion includes including specific Docker commands that facilitate this process.

## Helpful background
The issue stems from the lack of clear guidance in the [plone-backend container documentation](https://github.com/plone/documentation/blob/6-dev/docs/install/containers/images/backend.md). Community input suggests that users often look for reliable ways to install add-ons in a production setting, thus making accurate documentation essential.

## Good first steps
1. Review the existing documentation linked above.
2. Implement the suggested Docker commands for adding add-ons properly.
3. Ensure any new documentation is easily understandable, especially for newcomers.
4. Consider adding examples and testing procedures if applicable.
5. Submit your changes for review to get feedback and make improvements.
```
